import LightColors from '@site/src/components/Docs/Colors/LightColors';

The [`BaseTheme<TColors>`](./theme-provider.mdx#basethemetcolors) theme object is where you define your application's color palette, type scale, borders, border radius values, and more.

:::info
First of all you need to install `@flexnative/theme-context`.
Fallow instructions here on **[how to install and how to use](../getting-started/installation.mdx)** `@flexnative/theme-context`.
:::

## Colors
Default theme colors. You can extend the `BaseColors` with your own color properties.

<LightColors />

## Spaces
Spacing values for layout, such as margins and paddings. This is a key part of the theme that helps maintain consistent spacing across components.

|  Name  | Type | Value | Description |
| ------ | ---- | ----- | ----------- |
| none | `number` | 0 | No spacing. |
| xxxs | `number` | 2 | Spacing of 2 units. |
| xxs | `number` | 4 | Spacing of 4 units. |
| xs | `number` | 6 | Spacing of 6 units. |
| sm | `number` | 8 | Spacing of 8 units. |
| md | `number` | 12 | Spacing of 12 units. |
| default | `number` | 16 | Spacing of 16 units. |
| lg | `number` | 18 | Spacing of 18 units. |
| xl | `number` | 24 | Spacing of 24 units. |
| xxl | `number` | 32 | Spacing of 32 units. |
| xxxl | `number` | 36 | Spacing of 36 units. |
| `number` | `number` |  | A custom numeric spacing value. |

## FontSize
Represents the possible font sizes used for text elements.

|  Name  | Type | Value | Description |
| ------ | ---- | ----- | ----------- |
| `xxs`| `number` | A small font size, 8 units. |
| `xs` | `number` | The default font size, 12 units. |
| `sm` | `number` | A medium font size, 14 units. |
| `md` | `number` | A large font size, 16 units. |
| `lg` | `number` | A large font size, 18 units. |
| `xl` | `number` | A large font size, 20 units. |
| `xxl`| `number` | A large font size, 24 units. |
| `number` | `number` | A custom numeric font size. |

### Borders
Border styles for components. This can include border widths, radius to ensure a consistent look and feel across your UI components.

### BorderWidth
Represents the possible border width values.

|  Name  | Type | Value | Description |
| ------ | ---- | ----- | ----------- |
| `none` | `number` | No border width (0). |
| `hairline` |`number` | A hairline border width, typically the thinnest possible line on the device. See [StyleSheet.hairlineWidth](https://reactnative.dev/docs/stylesheet#hairlinewidth). |
| `thin` | `number` | A thin border width, 1 unit. |
| `base` | `number` | A base border width, 2 unit2. |
| `thick` | `number` | A thick border width, 3 unit. |
| `number` | `number` | A custom numeric border width. |


### BorderRadius
Represents the possible border radius values.

|  Name  | Type | Value | Description |
| ------ | ---- | ----- | ----------- |
| `none` | `number` | No border radius (0). |
| `small` | `number` | A small border radius (2). |
| `medium` | `number` | A medium border radius (4). |
| `large` | `number` | A large border radius (6). |
| `full` | `number` | A fully rounded border radius (99999). |
| `number` | `number` | A custom numeric border radius. |
